title: Ragi Mudde (Finger Millet Balls)

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Water: 2 cups (for 1 cup flour; adjust slightly if needed)
Ghee (Clarified Butter): 1 tsp per serving (optional for finishing)

ingredients.title: Accompaniments (optional, restaurant style)
items: Spiced yogurt (curd with green chili, ginger, and curry leaves)

instruction.title: 1. Boil the water
steps: In a heavy-bottomed pan, bring 2 cups water to a rolling boil.
Add ½ tsp salt if desired.

instruction.title: 2. Prepare Ragi Flour
steps: Dry-roast 1 cup ragi flour lightly for 1–2 minutes in a pan (optional) to remove raw taste.

instruction.title: 3. Form the Mudde
steps: Gradually add the ragi flour into the boiling water while stirring continuously with a wooden spatula or whisk to avoid lumps.
Cook on low heat for 2–3 minutes, stirring continuously.

instruction.title: 4. Steam & Cook Thoroughly
steps: Cover and allow the mixture to cook on low heat for 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add a little water if the mixture feels too thick or dry while stirring.

instruction.title: 5. Shape into Balls
steps: Take small portions of the hot ragi dough and roll into smooth, round balls (size of a small fist, about 2–3 inches).

instruction.title: 6. Finishing Touch (optional)
steps: Drizzle a little ghee over the hot Mudde for a glossy, restaurant-style finish.

chefTips: Consistency: The ragi dough should be soft but firm enough to hold shape. Too stiff → dry balls; too soft → sticky.
Continuous stirring: Prevents lumps and ensures even cooking.
Serving style: Traditionally, Mudde is served on a banana leaf with sides like sambar, rasam, or spiced chutney.
Temperature: Serve hot – Ragi Mudde hardens as it cools.
